Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The event log has 3 cases Case 1 trace RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC Case 2 trace RA VF AE AD SC AC Case 3 trace RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets analyze the traces All traces start with RA Then VF Then AE

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: After AE theres an optional loop of RMI followed by VF

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets check if this matches the log Case 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC Matches where the loop executes VF once and exits Case 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC Matches where the loop executes VF then RMI then VF then exits

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait in Case 3 RMI RMI VF If its VF RMI RMI VF then the RMI is executed twice in a row This does NOT match This means this does NOT cover Case 3

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So after AE we either go to AD or we do RMI RMI then VF then AD How to model this XOR between going directly to AD or doing sequence with loop of RMI then VF then AD

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets check if this perfectly matches the log Case 1 AE Path 2 one RMI then VF then AD Case 2 AE Path 1 then AD Case 3 AE Path 2 two RMIs then VF then AD

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: What about sequence of loop tau RMI and choice tau VF For Case 2 zero RMIs and tau gives empty For Case 1 one RMI and VF gives RMI VF For Case 3 two RMIs and VF gives RMI RMI VF This works perfectly and is very elegant

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Possible traces from this model include empty VF RMI RMI VF RMI RMI and so on It allows RMI without a subsequent VF

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Could we make it more precise so that if theres at least one RMI there MUST be a VF We already found one X tau sequence loop RMI tau then VF This strictly requires that if any RMI is executed VF must be executed afterwards

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets reverify this one Case 2 chooses tau giving empty Case 1 chooses loop with one RMI then VF Case 3 chooses loop with two RMIs then VF This is exactly what happens in the log

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: This model is strictly more precise than the previous one because it forbids the dangling RMI and it is also simpler

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The whole tree is sequence RA VF AE choice tau or sequence loop RMI tau then VF then AD SC AC
